title: hieracium subramosum subsp. scotocranum
intro: Hieracium subramosum subsp. scotocranum, commonly known as the Scottish hawkweed, is a plant species within the dandelion-like genus Hieracium. This plant is known for its medicinal properties and has been used in traditional herbal medicine for various health benefits. The health benefits of Hieracium subramosum subsp. scotocranum include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and protect against infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects.
side_effects: Hieracium subramosum subsp. scotocranum can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. Prolonged consumption of the plant can also lead to allergic reactions, such as skin rashes and respiratory problems. In rare cases, excessive ingestion of Hieracium subramosum subsp. scotocranum may cause liver and kidney damage, potentially resulting in long-term health complications. It is essential to consult with a medical professional before using this plant for medicinal purposes to avoid any potential adverse effects.
